message:
fix(cli,core,host,vectordb): hoist --input-stdin guard, reject over-long byte arrays, log connector stderr, lazy JSON default

Four targeted production improvements:

1. fcp-cli/main.rs: Hoist per-command `if cli.input_stdin { bail!(...) }`
   guards into a single early check before the match dispatch block.
   The --input-stdin flag is only valid for `fcp doctor`, so checking once
   is cleaner than repeating in every non-doctor branch. Net -51 lines of
   redundant boilerplate.

2. fcp-core/util/hex_or_bytes.rs: Add overflow check in byte-array
   deserializer — after reading exactly N elements from a sequence, now
   checks for an (N+1)th element and returns invalid_length error instead
   of silently truncating. Prevents data corruption or adversarial
   payloads from being silently accepted.

3. fcp-host/bin/fcp-host.rs: In ConnectorProcessRunner, the stderr
   reader loop now logs non-empty lines from child connector processes
   via tracing::warn!(connector_stderr = %trimmed). Previously stderr
   was silently consumed, making connector issues invisible to operators.

4. connectors/vectordb/src/main.rs: Change .unwrap_or(json!({})) to
   .unwrap_or_else(|| json!({})) to avoid eagerly allocating an empty
   JSON object on every call (lazy evaluation).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
